title: By the Numbers
text:
Background
I created this as a fun exercise to help motivate my local club members to get more involved in Field Day. That entailed a deep dive into the club’s past involvement in Field Day with a view toward setting goals for future events. Data about our club’s activity can be found here.
After some exploration of the data focused on the club, I realized that the broader dataset might be of interest to others.






ImportantWork in progress



This is a work in-progress.
Results may change without notice.

title: By the Numbers
text: Data from 2000-2009 are available in PDF form but must be converted to text data before they can be included.↩︎

title: Gather
text:
About
This page documents the process of gathering Field Day data from the ARRL website.

title: Gather
text:
Download
The ARRL has comma-separated value (text) files for Field Day results here: https://field-day.arrl.org/fdresults.php.
CSV data are available for 2010-2025.






NoteFuture work



QST articles go back to 2000, so the results from 2000-2009 could eventually be extracted from these PDF files and turned into actionable data.


Create functions to download the data given year as an input.


Code
download_fd_data <- function(year = 2025) {
  assertthat::is.number(year)
  assertthat::assert_that(year > 0)
  
  url <- paste0("https://contests.arrl.org/ContestResults/", year, "/field-day-", year, ".csv")
  readr::read_csv(url, show_col_types = FALSE)
}




Code
save_fd_data <- function(df, year, data_dir = "data/raw", force_create_dir = TRUE) {
  assertthat::assert_that(tibble::is_tibble(df))
  
  assertthat::is.number(year)
  assertthat::assert_that(year > 0)
  
  assertthat::is.string(data_dir)
  assertthat::assert_that(is.logical(force_create_dir))
  if (!assertthat::is.writeable(data_dir)) {
    if (force_create_dir) {
      dir.create(data_dir)
    } else {
      warning(paste0(data_dir, " does not exist. Exiting."))
      return()
    }
  }
  
  fn <- file.path(data_dir, paste0("field-day-", year, ".csv"))
  readr::write_csv(df, fn)
} 




Code
download_save_df_data <- function(year = 2025) {
  df <- download_fd_data(year)
  save_fd_data(df, year)
}


With these functions, we can use the {purrr} package to download and save all of the available data files.


Code
years <- c(2010:2025)
purrr::map(years, download_save_df_data, .progress = TRUE)

title: Clean
text:
About
This page documents the process of cleaning the 2010-2025 ARRL Field Day data.
The datasets have different field names across the years, so the field names must be normalized in order to combine the datasets for visualization.

title: Clean
text:
Tidy names


Code
clean_fd_names_save <- function(fn, clean_file_dir = "data/cleaned") {
  assertthat::is.string(fn)
  assertthat::is.readable(fn)
  
  df <- readr::read_csv(fn, show_col_types = FALSE)
  assertthat::assert_that(tibble::is_tibble(df))
  
  # Clean names
  df_clean <- janitor::clean_names(df)

  fn_no_ext <- basename(tools::file_path_sans_ext(fn))
  fn <- file.path(clean_file_dir, paste0(fn_no_ext, "-clean-names.csv"))
  readr::write_csv(df_clean, fn)
  fn
}


Clean all names for all files in data/raw.


Code
fl <- list.files("data/raw", full.names = TRUE)

purrr::map(fl, clean_fd_names_save)

title: Clean
text:
Exploration
Let’s explore the column names to see which ones represent the same data.


Code
show_names <- function(fn) {
  assertthat::is.string(fn)
  assertthat::is.readable(fn)
  
  df <- readr::read_csv(fn, show_col_types = FALSE)
  assertthat::assert_that(tibble::is_tibble(df))
  file_year <- stringr::str_extract(fn, pattern = "[0-9]{4}")
  sprintf("%s\n", paste0("--- ", file_year, " ---"))
  names(df)
}

fl <- list.files("data/cleaned", pattern = "clean-names", full.names = TRUE)

purrr::map(fl, show_names)

title: Clean
text:
Merge cleaned


Code
import_add_year <- function(fn) {
  assertthat::is.string(fn)
  assertthat::is.readable(fn)
  
  df <- readr::read_csv(fn, show_col_types = FALSE)
  assertthat::assert_that(tibble::is_tibble(df))
  df$year <- stringr::str_extract(fn, "[0-9]{4}")
  df
}
fl <- list.files("data/cleaned", pattern = "normed", full.names = TRUE)

fd_all_df <- purrr::map(fl, import_add_year) |>
  purrr::list_rbind()

readr::write_csv(fd_all_df, file = "data/aggregate/field-day-2010-2025.csv")

title: Visualize
text:
About
This page documents visualizations of the ARRL Field Day 2010-2025 data.

title: Visualize
text:
Setup
Here I source the {ggplot2} library to make it easier to make plots later. I also define some default colors for those plots. Defining these here makes it easier to modify them site-wide later on.


Code
library(ggplot2)

max_color = "red"
median_color = "blue"
tot_color = "green"

title: Visualize
text:
Import
The gathering and cleaning pages handle those functions. They save a cleaned data file that we reimport here.






ImportantDRY WIT



A popular adage in computer science and data science is DRY WIT: Don’t Repeat Yourself; Write It Down.
I adopt that mantra here by automating each step and by saving the intermediate products.




Code
fd <- readr::read_csv("data/aggregate/field-day-2010-2025.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

title: Visualize
text:
Generate summaries
To begin, we create a smaller data summary.


Code
fd_by_year <- fd |>
  dplyr::group_by(year) |>
  dplyr::summarize(
    median_score = median(final_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_score = max(final_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    tot_score = sum(final_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_xmtrs = median(transmitters),
    max_xmtrs = max(transmitters),
    median_particip = median(number_of_participants, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_particip = max(number_of_participants, na.rm = TRUE),
    tot_particip = sum(number_of_participants, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_qsos = median(total_qsos, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_qsos = max(total_qsos, na.rm = TRUE),
    tot_qsos = sum(total_qsos, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_stations = length(unique(call))
  )

title: Visualize
text:
Scores by year
This section visualizes Field Day scores across the 2010-2025 period.

MedianMaximumMaximum & medianTotal




Code
fd_by_year |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_point(aes(x = year, y = median_score)) +
  geom_line(aes(x = year, y = median_score), color = median_color) +
  ylab("Median score")

title: By the Numbers
text:
About
Welcome to my Field Day data site.
Separate pages describe how I gathered the data, cleaned it, and generated visualizations.
The data come from the ARRL, and for now1 cover 2010-2025.
